claim: Custom tools are the secret sauce of agent quality, more important than framework choice or LLM selection
actionable: Invest heavily in well-designed tool specifications (names, descriptions, input/output formatting) over framework optimization.

claim: Tool names and descriptions must be extremely clear (e.g., 'read_posts_from_subreddit' not 'Reddit_tool')
actionable: Name tools with specific action verbs and objects: use 'get_weather_forecast_for_location' not 'weather_tool'.

claim: Custom tools must handle three categories: data getters (search, API, RAG), data manipulators (math, planning), action takers (UI, files)
actionable: Classify each tool into one of three categories to ensure diverse capability coverage and clearer orchestration logic.

claim: Tools must do input validation and API adaptation, not just make raw API calls or pass raw HTML to LLM
actionable: Tools must wrap API calls with: (1) validate LLM output before API call, (2) format API response as concise summary not raw data.

claim: Verification/checker tools (code review, unit tests, logic validators) are critical for agent reliability
actionable: Always include dedicated verification tools (not just action tools): run tests on generated code, validate JSON, check logic.

claim: Build a reusable tool library across projects; track ~50 tools with documentation by use case and LLM framework
actionable: Maintain a documented tool repository with version control; categorize by domain and framework to enable rapid agent assembly.
